topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The studio air felt thick, humming with a low, persistent thrumming that vibrated in my bones. I was meticulously cleaning my brushes, the rhythmic scrape of bristles against ceramic a deliberate counterpoint to the restless energy coiled inside me. My gaze swept across the canvas resting on the easel—a swirling depiction of a storm-tossed sea. As I leaned closer to examine the texture, a detail snagged my attention: a tiny, almost invisible line of ink tracing the edge of a wave crest. It wasn't part of my composition. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, and I straightened up, my breath catching in my throat. Someone had marked my work.

[story 2]

I paced the length of the loft, my movements sharp and jerky, every muscle taut beneath my worn denim shirt. The silence of the early morning was oppressive, amplifying the frantic drumming behind my eyes. I ran a hand over my jaw, feeling the slight tremor that ran through my fingertips. When I finally turned back to the easel, the vibrant colors of my latest portrait seemed suddenly aggressive. There, beneath the ochre of the sunset, was a precise, intricate pattern—a tattoo. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drum solo. I stopped dead, my boots skidding slightly on the wooden floor. Who had dared to claim a piece of my vision?

[story 3]

A strange tension clung to the room, a prickly static that made the fine hairs on my arms stand on end. I kept tapping my pen against my notepad, the sound a rapid, insistent rhythm against the quiet. My focus, usually a smooth current, had fractured. I moved toward the large, unfinished sculpture, my steps deliberate but quick, scanning the surface as if expecting an intruder. Then I saw it—a deliberate, stylized marking on the base, a permanent addition that wasn't mine. A slow, chilling realization washed over me, leaving a hollow sensation in my chest. Someone had claimed ownership of my creation.
